sn42.10:1.1.pli: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
sn42.10:1.1.eng: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.

sn42.10:1.2.pli: Tena kho pana samayena rājantepure rājaparisāya sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
sn42.10:1.2.eng: Now at that time while the king’s retinue was sitting together in the royal compound this discussion came up among them,

sn42.10:1.3.pli: “kappati samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtiyānaṁ jātarūparajataṁ, sādiyanti samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jātarūparajataṁ, paṭiggaṇhanti samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jātarūparajatan”ti.
sn42.10:1.3.eng: “Gold and currency are proper for ascetics who follow the Sakyan. They accept and receive gold and currency.”

sn42.10:2.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena maṇicūḷako gāmaṇi tassaṁ parisāyaṁ nisinno hoti.
sn42.10:2.1.eng: Now at that time the chief Maṇicūḷaka was sitting in that assembly.

sn42.10:2.2.pli: Atha kho maṇicūḷako gāmaṇi taṁ parisaṁ etadavoca:

sn42.10:2.4.pli: Na kappati samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtiyānaṁ jātarūparajataṁ, na sādiyanti samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jātarūparajataṁ, nappaṭiggaṇhanti samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jātarūparajataṁ, nikkhittamaṇisuvaṇṇā samaṇā sakyaputtiyā apetajātarūparajatā”ti.
sn42.10:2.4.eng: Gold and currency are not proper for ascetics who follow the Sakyan. They neither accept nor receive gold and currency. They have set aside gems and gold, and quit using gold and currency.”

sn42.10:2.5.pli: Asakkhi kho maṇicūḷako gāmaṇi taṁ parisaṁ saññāpetuṁ.
sn42.10:2.5.eng: He was able to persuade that assembly.

sn42.10:2.6.pli: Atha kho maṇicūḷako gāmaṇi yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho maṇicūḷako gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn42.10:2.6.eng: Then Maṇicūḷaka went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened. He then said,

sn42.10:2.7.pli: “idha, bhante, rājantepure rājaparisāya sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:

sn42.10:2.8.pli: ‘kappati samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtiyānaṁ jātarūparajataṁ, sādiyanti samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jātarūparajataṁ, paṭiggaṇhanti samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jātarūparajatan’ti.

sn42.10:2.9.pli: Evaṁ vutte, ahaṁ, bhante, taṁ parisaṁ etadavocaṁ:

sn42.10:2.11.pli: Na kappati samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtiyānaṁ jātarūparajataṁ, na sādiyanti samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jātarūparajataṁ, nappaṭiggaṇhanti samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jātarūparajataṁ, nikkhittamaṇisuvaṇṇā samaṇā sakyaputtiyā apetajātarūparajatā’ti.

sn42.10:2.12.pli: Asakkhiṁ khvāhaṁ, bhante, taṁ parisaṁ saññāpetuṁ.

sn42.10:2.13.pli: Kaccāhaṁ, bhante, evaṁ byākaramāno vuttavādī ceva bhagavato homi, na ca bhagavantaṁ abhūtena abbhācikkhāmi, dhammassa cānudhammaṁ byākaromi, na ca koci sahadhammiko vādānuvādo gārayhaṁ ṭhānaṁ āgacchatī”ti?
sn42.10:2.13.eng: “Answering this way, I trust that I repeat what the Buddha has said, and don’t misrepresent him with an untruth. I trust my explanation is in line with the teaching, and that there are no legitimate grounds for rebuttal or criticism.”

sn42.10:3.1.pli: “Taggha tvaṁ, gāmaṇi, evaṁ byākaramāno vuttavādī ceva me hosi, na ca maṁ abhūtena abbhācikkhasi, dhammassa cānudhammaṁ byākarosi, na ca koci sahadhammiko vādānuvādo gārayhaṁ ṭhānaṁ āgacchati.
sn42.10:3.1.eng: “Indeed, in answering this way you repeat what I’ve said, and don’t misrepresent me with an untruth. Your explanation is in line with the teaching, and there are no legitimate grounds for rebuttal or criticism.

sn42.10:3.2.pli: Na hi, gāmaṇi, kappati samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtiyānaṁ jātarūparajataṁ, na sādiyanti samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jātarūparajataṁ, nappaṭiggaṇhanti samaṇā sakyaputtiyā jātarūparajataṁ, nikkhittamaṇisuvaṇṇā samaṇā sakyaputtiyā apetajātarūparajatā.
sn42.10:3.2.eng: Gold and currency are not proper for ascetics who follow the Sakyan. They neither accept nor receive gold and currency. They have set aside gems and gold, and quit using gold and currency.

sn42.10:3.3.pli: Yassa kho, gāmaṇi, jātarūparajataṁ kappati, pañcapi tassa kāmaguṇā kappanti.
sn42.10:3.3.eng: If gold and currency were proper for them, then the five kinds of sensual stimulation would also be proper.

sn42.10:3.4.pli: Yassa pañca kāmaguṇā kappanti (…), ekaṁsenetaṁ, gāmaṇi, dhāreyyāsi assamaṇadhammo asakyaputtiyadhammoti.
sn42.10:3.4.eng: And if the five kinds of sensual stimulation are proper for them, you should categorically regard them as not having the qualities of an ascetic or a follower of the Sakyan.

sn42.10:3.6.pli: tiṇaṁ tiṇatthikena pariyesitabbaṁ, dāru dārutthikena pariyesitabbaṁ, sakaṭaṁ sakaṭatthikena pariyesitabbaṁ, puriso purisatthikena pariyesitabbo.
sn42.10:3.6.eng: Straw may be looked for by one needing straw; wood may be looked for by one needing wood; a cart may be looked for by one needing a cart; a workman may be looked for by one needing a workman.

sn42.10:3.7.pli: Na tvevāhaṁ, gāmaṇi, kenaci pariyāyena ‘jātarūparajataṁ sāditabbaṁ pariyesitabban’ti vadāmī”ti.
sn42.10:3.7.eng: But in any case, there is no way they can accept or look for gold and currency, I say .”

sn42.11:1.1.pli: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā mallesu viharati uruvelakappaṁ nāma mallānaṁ nigamo.
sn42.11:1.1.eng: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Mallas, near the Mallian town named Uruvelakappa.

sn42.11:1.2.pli: Atha kho bhadrako gāmaṇi yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho bhadrako gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn42.11:1.2.eng: Then Bhadraka the village chief went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:

sn42.11:1.3.pli: “sādhu me, bhante, bhagavā dukkhassa samudayañca atthaṅgamañca desetū”ti.
sn42.11:1.3.eng: “Please, sir, let the Blessed One teach me the origin and cessation of suffering.”

sn42.11:1.4.pli: “Ahañce te, gāmaṇi, atītamaddhānaṁ ārabbha dukkhassa samudayañca atthaṅgamañca deseyyaṁ:
sn42.11:1.4.eng: “Chief, if I were to teach you about the origin and disappearance of suffering in the past, saying

sn42.11:1.5.pli: ‘evaṁ ahosi atītamaddhānan’ti, tatra te siyā kaṅkhā, siyā vimati.
sn42.11:1.5.eng: ‘this is how it was in the past,’ you might have doubts or uncertainties about that.

sn42.11:1.6.pli: Ahañce te, gāmaṇi, anāgatamaddhānaṁ ārabbha dukkhassa samudayañca atthaṅgamañca deseyyaṁ:
sn42.11:1.6.eng: If I were to teach you about the origin and disappearance of suffering in the future, saying

sn42.11:1.7.pli: ‘evaṁ bhavissati anāgatamaddhānan’ti, tatrāpi te siyā kaṅkhā, siyā vimati.
sn42.11:1.7.eng: ‘this is how it will be in the future,’ you might have doubts or uncertainties about that.

sn42.11:1.8.pli: Api cāhaṁ, gāmaṇi, idheva nisinno ettheva te nisinnassa dukkhassa samudayañca atthaṅgamañca desessāmi.
sn42.11:1.8.eng: Rather, chief, I will teach you about the origin and disappearance of suffering as I am sitting right here and you are sitting right there.

sn42.11:1.9.pli: Taṁ suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
sn42.11:1.9.eng: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”

sn42.11:1.10.pli: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho bhadrako gāmaṇi bhagavato paccassosi.

sn42.11:2.2.pli: atthi te uruvelakappe manussā yesaṁ te vadhena vā bandhena vā jāniyā vā garahāya vā uppajjeyyuṁ sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā”ti?
sn42.11:2.2.eng: Are there any people here in Uruvelakappa who, if they were executed, imprisoned, fined, or condemned, it would cause you sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress?”

sn42.11:2.3.pli: “Atthi me, bhante, uruvelakappe manussā yesaṁ me vadhena vā bandhena vā jāniyā vā garahāya vā uppajjeyyuṁ sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā”ti.

sn42.11:2.4.pli: “Atthi pana te, gāmaṇi, uruvelakappe manussā yesaṁ te vadhena vā bandhena vā jāniyā vā garahāya vā nuppajjeyyuṁ sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā”ti?
sn42.11:2.4.eng: “But are there any people here in Uruvelakappa who, if they were executed, imprisoned, fined, or condemned, it would not cause you sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress?”

sn42.11:2.5.pli: “Atthi me, bhante, uruvelakappe manussā yesaṁ me vadhena vā bandhena vā jāniyā vā garahāya vā nuppajjeyyuṁ sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā”ti.

sn42.11:2.6.pli: “Ko nu kho, gāmaṇi, hetu, ko paccayo yena te ekaccānaṁ uruvelakappiyānaṁ manussānaṁ vadhena vā bandhena vā jāniyā vā garahāya vā uppajjeyyuṁ sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā”ti?
sn42.11:2.6.eng: “What’s the cause, chief, what’s the reason why, if this was to happen to some people it could cause you sorrow, while if it happens to others it does not?”

sn42.11:2.7.pli: “Yesaṁ me, bhante, uruvelakappiyānaṁ manussānaṁ vadhena vā bandhena vā jāniyā vā garahāya vā uppajjeyyuṁ sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā, atthi me tesu chandarāgo.
sn42.11:2.7.eng: “The people regarding whom this would give rise to sorrow are those I have desire and passion.

sn42.11:2.8.pli: Yesaṁ pana, bhante, uruvelakappiyānaṁ manussānaṁ vadhena vā bandhena vā jāniyā vā garahāya vā nuppajjeyyuṁ sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā, natthi me tesu chandarāgo”ti.
sn42.11:2.8.eng: The people regarding whom this would not give rise to sorrow are those I don’t have desire and passion.”

sn42.11:2.9.pli: “Iminā tvaṁ, gāmaṇi, dhammena diṭṭhena viditena akālikena pattena pariyogāḷhena atītānāgate nayaṁ nehi:
sn42.11:2.9.eng: “With this present phenomenon that you have seen, understood, immediately attained, and fathomed, you can apply the same method to the past and future:

sn42.11:2.10.pli: ‘yaṁ kho kiñci atītamaddhānaṁ dukkhaṁ uppajjamānaṁ uppajji sabbaṁ taṁ chandamūlakaṁ chandanidānaṁ.
sn42.11:2.10.eng: ‘All the suffering that arose in the past was rooted and sourced in desire.

sn42.11:2.11.eng: For desire is the root of suffering.

sn42.11:2.12.pli: Yampi hi kiñci anāgatamaddhānaṁ dukkhaṁ uppajjamānaṁ uppajjissati, sabbaṁ taṁ chandamūlakaṁ chandanidānaṁ.
sn42.11:2.12.eng: All the suffering that will arise in the future will be rooted and sourced in desire.

sn42.11:2.13.eng: For desire is the root of suffering.’”

sn42.11:2.15.eng: How well said this was by the Buddha!

sn42.11:2.16.pli: ‘yaṁ kiñci dukkhaṁ uppajjamānaṁ uppajjati, sabbaṁ taṁ chandamūlakaṁ chandanidānaṁ.
sn42.11:2.16.eng: ‘All the suffering that arises is rooted and sourced in desire.

sn42.11:2.17.eng: For desire is the root of suffering.’

sn42.11:2.18.pli: Atthi me, bhante, ciravāsī nāma kumāro bahi āvasathe paṭivasati.
sn42.11:2.18.eng: I have a boy called Ciravāsi, who resides in a house away from here.

sn42.11:2.19.pli: So khvāhaṁ, bhante, kālasseva vuṭṭhāya purisaṁ uyyojemi:
sn42.11:2.19.eng: I rise early and dispatch someone, saying:

sn42.11:2.20.eng: ‘Go, my man, and check on my boy Ciravāsi.’

sn42.11:2.21.pli: Yāvakīvañca, bhante, so puriso nāgacchati, tassa me hoteva aññathattaṁ:
sn42.11:2.21.eng: Until they get back I worry:

sn42.11:3.2.pli: ciravāsissa kumārassa vadhena vā bandhena vā jāniyā vā garahāya vā uppajjeyyuṁ sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā”ti?
sn42.11:3.2.eng: If Ciravāsi was executed, imprisoned, fined, or condemned, would it cause you sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress?”

sn42.11:3.3.pli: “Ciravāsissa me, bhante, kumārassa vadhena vā bandhena vā jāniyā vā garahāya vā jīvitassapi siyā aññathattaṁ, kiṁ pana me nuppajjissanti sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā”ti.

sn42.11:3.4.eng: “This too is a way to understand:

sn42.11:3.5.pli: ‘yaṁ kiñci dukkhaṁ uppajjamānaṁ uppajjati, sabbaṁ taṁ chandamūlakaṁ chandanidānaṁ.
sn42.11:3.5.eng: ‘All the suffering that arises is rooted and sourced in desire.

sn42.11:3.6.eng: For desire is the root of suffering.’

sn42.11:4.2.pli: yadā te ciravāsimātā adiṭṭhā ahosi, assutā ahosi, te ciravāsimātuyā chando vā rāgo vā pemaṁ vā”ti?
sn42.11:4.2.eng: Before you’d seen or heard of Ciravāsi’s mother, did you have any desire or passion or fondness for her?”

sn42.11:4.4.pli: “Dassanaṁ vā te, gāmaṇi, āgamma savanaṁ vā evaṁ te ahosi:
sn42.11:4.4.eng: “Then was it because you saw or heard of her that you had

sn42.11:4.5.pli: ‘ciravāsimātuyā chando vā rāgo vā pemaṁ vā’”ti?
sn42.11:4.5.eng: desire or passion or fondness for her?”

sn42.11:5.2.pli: ciravāsimātuyā te vadhena vā bandhena vā jāniyā vā garahāya vā uppajjeyyuṁ sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā”ti?
sn42.11:5.2.eng: If Ciravāsi’s mother was executed, imprisoned, fined, or condemned, would it cause you sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress?”

sn42.11:5.3.pli: “Ciravāsimātuyā me, bhante, vadhena vā bandhena vā jāniyā vā garahāya vā jīvitassapi siyā aññathattaṁ, kiṁ pana me nuppajjissanti sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā”ti.

sn42.11:5.4.eng: “This too is a way to understand:

sn42.11:5.5.pli: ‘yaṁ kiñci dukkhaṁ uppajjamānaṁ uppajjati, sabbaṁ taṁ chandamūlakaṁ chandanidānaṁ.
sn42.11:5.5.eng: ‘All the suffering that arises is rooted and sourced in desire.

sn42.11:5.6.eng: For desire is the root of suffering.’”
